summary:
Claim: The primary site of lesion in rheumatic heart disease endocarditis is along the lines of closure of the valve leaflets.  
Evidence: - Commissural zones experience high shear stress and immune infiltration due to biomechanical vulnerability. - These areas show preferential deposition of immune complexes and fibrin, initiating localized inflammation and scarring.  
Answer: A
